title: Licensing Life-Saving Drugs for Developing Countries: Evidence from the Medicines Patent Pool

construct_vars: gvar_df <- df[df$MPP == 1, c('ID', 'year')]; gvar_df <- aggregate(year ~ ID, data=gvar_df, FUN=min); names(gvar_df)[2] <- 'gvar_CS'

notes: Staggered DiD: 6746 country×product units, 14 years (2005-2018). 7 treatment cohorts (2011-2017). 84.6% never-treated. Treatment = Medicines Patent Pool membership. Outcome = drug access (binary). Stata uses two-way clustering (product_code × country_code) but template uses one-way (product_code). Unbalanced panel (84.8% fill rate). Bacon decomposition initially disabled due to claimed large dataset size (incorrect — 7-cohort design = 28 two-by-two pairs). FIX 2026-04-20: run_bacon toggled false→true. | FIX 2026-04-19: allow_unbalanced toggled false→true. With 84.8% fill rate and severe cohort imbalance, did_standarization balancing failed → NA. With allow_unbalanced=true: CS-NT=0.7135 (direction matches TWFE 0.6625, +7.7% magnitude). Paper-auditor EXACT (0.07%).
